directive.title: RFQ detail page loading skeleton and error boundary

auditor.summary: The build implements the skeleton component and error card UI correctly, but has critical architectural issues. Converting the page from a server component to a client component breaks Next.js 14 App Router patterns and will cause existing tests to fail. The import path for ErrorBoundary is incorrect per the build specification. The async component inside a 'use client' file is invalid. The Playwright tests have minor flaws in timing assertions and retry verification. The build needs significant restructuring to maintain server component architecture while adding Suspense and error boundary wrappers.

top_issues.description: The page is converted from a server component to a client component by adding 'use client' directive. This breaks the existing architecture where app/rfq/[id]/page.tsx was a server component. The build spec says 'Do not restructure or move the existing /api/rfq/[id] fetch logic — only wrap it', but converting to client component fundamentally restructures the data fetching pattern. Additionally, the async RFQDetailContent component inside a 'use client' file is invalid — Next.js 14 does not support async server components inside client component files.

BUILDABILITY violation: The build spec requires using 'lib/error-boundary (AUTO-014)' but the generated code imports from '../../../components/ui/ErrorBoundary'. The spec explicitly states 'Use existing lib/error-boundary export from AUTO-014 without modification'. The import path is incorrect and the component may not exist at the expected location.

The async RFQDetailContent component is defined inside a 'use client' file. In Next.js 14 App Router, async components are only supported in server components. This will cause a runtime error or unexpected behavior.
